TokenConfig.title: TokenConfig
TokenConfig.description:
Credential configuration for upstream MCP servers.

The broker resolves the upstream credential via ``provider``:

- ``passthrough`` (default): forward the caller's Trimble Identity
  JWT to the upstream unchanged. This is what every real-server
  direct call gets today.
- ``token-vault``: exchange the caller's JWT for a 3rd-party
  credential stored in Token Vault.

Virtual servers additionally perform a per-source RFC 8693 OAuth
2.0 Token Exchange on every ``tools/call`` fan-out when the broker
is configured with a ``TokenExchangeProvider`` (i.e.
``MCP_BROKER_TOKEN_EXCHANGE_ENABLED=true`` and the OAuth client
credentials are wired up). That downscoping is the platform
default for virtual-server tool invocations and is not
configurable per-source: composition implies consent, and the
broker always minimises the credential reaching each source.

ToolConfig.title: ToolConfig
ToolConfig.description:
Per-tool configuration within a virtual server source.

Mirrors the agent-svc McpToolConfigDocument model for cross-platform
consistency.

allowed.title: Allowed
allowed.description: Whether unlisted tools are included (true) or blocked (false). When false, only tools explicitly listed with allowed=true are exposed.

ToolDefaults.title: ToolDefaults
ToolDefaults.description: Default settings for tools not explicitly listed in the tools array.

ToolFilter.title: ToolFilter
ToolFilter.description:
Bulk include/exclude operations by tags, paths, or operationIds.

Translated to fastmcp RouteMap rules at parse time.

AAIP-2187: ``include_paths`` / ``exclude_paths`` are interpreted as
regex by the parse worker (``apps/common/openapi_parser.py``
``_compile_include_patterns`` and ``build_route_maps``). Validating
the regexes at admission time blocks ReDoS-shaped patterns from
reaching the runtime, where a catastrophic backtrack would pin
a tool_registry CPU thread during ``FastMCP.from_openapi``.

name.title: Name
name.description: Renames the tool to a friendlier alias. Original name is used for matching.

description.title: Description
description.description: Replaces the upstream tool description when set.

input_schema.title: Input Schema
input_schema.description: JSON Schema fragment deep-merged into the upstream tool's inputSchema.  Nested dicts are merged recursively; leaf values overwrite.

ToolOverride.title: ToolOverride
ToolOverride.description:
Per-tool overrides: rename, description replacement, and inputSchema merge.

When applied at runtime the broker renames the tool if ``name`` is set,
replaces the description if ``description`` is set, and deep-merges
``input_schema`` into the upstream tool's ``inputSchema``.

effective_required_scopes.description: Read-only. The sorted union of scopes a caller must present on their Trimble JWT to invoke any tool on this virtual server: ``{"mcp"}`` plus the virtual server's explicit ``required_scopes`` (or the path-derived fallback when empty) plus every source's ``required_scopes``. VirtualServerOut.description:
API response model for a virtual server.

'members' is excluded from serialization -- internal field only.

server_id.title: Server Id
server_id.description: Stable UUID of a registered real MCP server or OpenAPI server.

tool_defaults.description: Default settings for tools not explicitly listed. When tool_defaults.allowed is true (default), all discovered tools are included unless explicitly blocked. Must be left at the default when ``server_id`` references an OpenAPI server.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: Per-tool configuration. Each entry can allow/block a tool and optionally override its description or input schema. Tools not listed here fall back to tool_defaults. Capped at 50 entries per source. Must be empty when ``server_id`` references an OpenAPI server -- the OpenAPI server's own tools/tool_filter configuration is the single source of truth.

required_scopes.title: Required Scopes
required_scopes.description: Read-only. The OAuth scopes this source real server requires, snapshotted from the real server's own ``required_scopes`` field when the virtual server is synced. Any value supplied on a create or update request is ignored and overwritten at persist time. Use this to discover, from a virtual server's response, which scopes each composed backend contributes to the caller's required token.

VirtualServerSource.title: VirtualServerSource
VirtualServerSource.description:
One backend server contributing tools to this virtual server.

References the source server by its stable UUID so that renaming the
source server's path does not break the virtual server configuration.
A source may be a registered real MCP server or an OpenAPI server.

OpenAPI sources have a hard constraint: ``tools`` MUST be empty and
``tool_defaults`` MUST be the default (``allowed=True``). The OpenAPI
server's own ``tools[]`` (with ``overrides``), ``tool_filter`` and
``route_maps`` configuration is the single source of truth for
filtering and tool overrides -- per-source overrides on top of an
OpenAPI source would be a confusing second layer of mutation and are
rejected at admission. Filtering or renaming for an OpenAPI source
must be configured on the OpenAPI server itself.

VirtualServerUpdate.title: VirtualServerUpdate
VirtualServerUpdate.description:
Partial update for a virtual server.

- Set 'status' to change lifecycle state ('deleted'/'deprecated' removes the Redis route).
- Set 'enabled' to enable or disable routing.
